I agree with option D because it is the most appropriate next step to assess fetal well-being comprehensively—especially given maternal diabetes increases risk for fetal compromise. In this case, a biophysical profile offers better diagnostic accuracy compared to repeat NSTs which may yield false negatives due to poor baseline variability in diabetic pregnancies.
